title: Pleurothallis tenera
taxonomy: Pleurothallis tenera is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which includes more than 30,000 species of orchids. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Pleurothallidinae subfamily to which Pleurothallis tenera belongs. The Pleurothallidinae subfamily is a group of terrestrial orchids native to Central and South America. The genus Pleurothallis includes around 600 species of orchids, most of which are found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. Pleurothallis tenera is a species within this diverse genus, characterized by its unique morphological features.

intro: Pleurothallis tenera, also known as the delicate Pleurothallis, is a species of orchid native to tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis tenera include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
